text: Initial scoping with CalyxBio to understand model-fit for genomics annotation pipelines. Customer needs long-context for full-read transcripts (~100k tokens in batches), multilingual support for research collaborators, and robust tool-calling (database lookups + lineage provenance). Discussed tradeoffs between Redwood Hosted for quick iteration, Dedicated for predictable throughput, and Private (VPC/on-prem) for data governance. Agreed to run a two-week bench: sample prompts, latency/cost spreadsheet, and an on-prem pilot scope for legal review.

title: Silvergate inference surge reconciliation and SLA commitment
preview: Readout from post-incident bridge: Silvergate experienced a multi-region inference surge causing elevated tail latency and partial fallback to smaller model variants. Redwood provided initial mitigation (fallback routing and temporary reservation). Discussion focused on root cause (unexpected burst pattern + cold KV...
text: Readout from post-incident bridge: Silvergate experienced a multi-region inference surge causing elevated tail latency and partial fallback to smaller model variants. Redwood provided initial mitigation (fallback routing and temporary reservation). Discussion focused on root cause (unexpected burst pattern + cold KV cache + kernel fallback on quantized paths), agreed next steps for trace sharing, a controlled re-run, and SLA amendment language for burst shielding.

title: POC traffic thermography + SLA definition - GraniteBank

text: Discovery and POC scoping focused on workload characterization for a 4-week pilot. Key metrics: baseline QPS ~12, expected peak bursts to 180-230 QPS, concurrency target 40-70, average input tokens ~35-60 and average model output tokens ~220-380. Success criteria: P99 generation latency under 350ms for interactive flows, 99.9% availability during pilot, cost envelope within provided budget. Bench plan: replay production traces in week 2, run controlled burst tests and tail latency characterization, deliver report and sizing recommendations by end of week 3.

title: Multi-NIC VPC Edge + Quantization: streaming fallbacks & p99 surface analysis - Catalyst Networks
preview: Deep technical session investigating intermittent p99 spikes observed when Catalyst routes streaming requests through the multi-NIC VPC edge. We covered how quantization changes execution paths, how KV cache hot-shards and network IRQ/coalescing cause tail amplification, and tradeoffs between streaming vs non-stream...
text: Deep technical session investigating intermittent p99 spikes observed when Catalyst routes streaming requests through the multi-NIC VPC edge. We covered how quantization changes execution paths, how KV cache hot-shards and network IRQ/coalescing cause tail amplification, and tradeoffs between streaming vs non-streaming fallbacks. Agreed to run targeted microbenchmarks single-vs-dual NIC and share benchmark spreadsheet; customer to provide traffic profile.
